evals.prompt: Create a browser extension project called notes-panel that shows a sidebar with a simple notes textarea that persists between sessions. It must work on both Chrome and Firefox from a single codebase, and clicking the toolbar icon should open the sidebar. Use vanilla TypeScript, no UI framework. Write all project files yourself (do not run package installs, scaffolding downloads, or browsers).
evals.expected_output: An extension.dev/Extension.js-convention project: src/manifest.json with chromium:/firefox: prefixed fields (side_panel vs sidebar_action, action vs browser_action, service_worker vs scripts, sidePanel permission only on chromium), a background script wiring the open trigger per browser family, sidebar HTML/TS entries, storage-backed persistence.
assertions: single-manifest-with-browser-prefixes: exactly one manifest.json covers both browsers, using chromium:/firefox: prefixed fields for divergent surfaces (side_panel vs sidebar_action, action vs browser_action, service_worker vs scripts) rather than two manifest copies or a custom build script
sidepanel-permission-chromium-only: the sidePanel permission is granted only to the Chromium family (e.g. chromium:permissions), not unprefixed and not on Firefox

open-trigger-wired-correctly: the background script wires an explicit open trigger per family, with chrome.sidePanel.setPanelBehavior (or equivalent) at the top level of the worker rather than inside a click handler, and a gesture-handler sidebarAction.open (or browser_action default popup/panel) path for Firefox
storage-backed-persistence: notes persist through the extension storage API (browser.storage.local or chrome.storage.local), not localStorage

extensionjs-project-conventions: project follows the framework layout (src/manifest.json as source of truth, manifest paths relative to src/, TypeScript entries referenced directly from manifest/HTML)

evals.prompt: I'm building a browser extension with extension.dev. My content script is supposed to inject a banner on example.com but nothing shows up on the page, and clicking the toolbar button also does nothing. There are no errors anywhere that I can see. Walk me through exactly how to figure out what is wrong, with concrete commands I can run. Write your full diagnostic plan to a file named DIAGNOSIS.md.
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis plan that uses the toolchain's feedback loop: --source with --source-probe (or extension_source_inspect) to verify injection, --logs (or extension_logs) for per-context console output, readiness gating, checking the action key / open trigger for the toolbar button, checking manifest matches and the dist manifest, rather than only generic advice to open DevTools.
assertions: uses-source-inspection-tooling: the plan verifies injection with the toolchain's live-DOM inspection (dev --source with --source-probe, or extension_source_inspect / extension_dom_inspect), not only manual DevTools clicking
uses-unified-log-streaming: the plan captures per-context console output via --logs (or extension_logs) covering background and content contexts

checks-toolbar-action-wiring: the plan addresses the dead toolbar button by checking the manifest action key and/or the open trigger (setPanelBehavior, onClicked listener, default_popup), or replays it via extension open action / extension_open
checks-manifest-matches-and-dist: the plan checks content_scripts matches patterns and/or diffs the built dist manifest against expectations
evidence-loop-not-guesswork: the plan is ordered as capture-evidence-then-change-code (readiness gating with --wait/ready.json counts), rather than proposing code changes before any observation

evals.prompt: Here is my extension's background service worker and manifest (in the input directory). Users report two bugs: (1) the visit counter resets to zero after the extension sits idle for a minute, and (2) lastUrl is sometimes undefined even on normal websites. Fix the code and explain each fix. Write the fixed files plus a short EXPLANATION.md to the output directory.
evals.expected_output: Fixed background script persisting state to chrome.storage instead of module variables, event listeners registered synchronously at the top level, tabs permission added to the manifest (tab.url is silently undefined without it), onMessage handler that only returns true when actually responding async, and an explanation citing the ~30s service worker idle termination.
assertions: state-persisted-to-storage: visitCount and lastUrl are persisted to chrome.storage (local or session) and re-read on wake; module-level variables are no longer the source of truth
listeners-registered-top-level: chrome.tabs.onActivated (and all other listeners) are registered synchronously at the top level, not inside onInstalled or a setTimeout
tabs-permission-added: the fixed manifest adds the tabs permission so tab.url is populated

onmessage-async-contract-fixed: the onMessage listener no longer unconditionally returns true while responding synchronously; it either responds async and returns true, or responds sync without returning true
explanation-cites-worker-lifetime: EXPLANATION.md attributes the counter reset to MV3 service worker idle termination (about 30 seconds) and the undefined lastUrl to the missing tabs permission
evals.notes: 2026-07-16 benchmark: this eval did not discriminate (baseline also 5/5); a strong base model already knows MV3 worker-lifetime fixes. Candidate for sharpening: require the fix to be verified through the toolchain loop, not just written.

evals.prompt: My Quote Keeper extension (in the input directory) is ready for users. I want to publish it to the Chrome Web Store, Firefox Add-ons, and Edge Add-ons. Get the project ready for store submission and write every file you create or change to the output directory.
evals.expected_output: A STORE.md at the project root following the skill's cross-store convention: shared listing and privacy sections above one section per store, Chrome permissions justifications matching the fixture manifest exactly (storage, contextMenus, activeTab, plus the <all_urls> content script match), Firefox reviewer/release notes, Edge certification notes, and a manifest updated with the Firefox data_collection_permissions declaration the fixture deliberately omits, agreeing with the privacy section.
assertions: creates-store-md-at-project-root: publishing intent triggers creation of a single STORE.md at the project root (not under src/), without the prompt naming the file or the convention

per-store-sections-present: STORE.md has one section per store (Chrome Web Store, Firefox Add-ons with reviewer notes and release notes, Edge Add-ons with certification notes) with shared listing and privacy material above them, matching the skill's store-md reference shape

justifications-match-manifest: every access the manifest actually requests gets a specific plain-English justification (storage, contextMenus, activeTab, and the <all_urls> content script match or host access), and no justification is invented for permissions the manifest does not request

adds-missing-data-collection-declaration: the manifest is updated to declare browser_specific_settings.gecko.data_collection_permissions (the fixture omits it), and the declared collection agrees with STORE.md's privacy section for a local-storage-only extension (none, or an equally consistent declaration)
reviewer-notes-cover-source-build: the Firefox reviewer notes account for AMO's bundled-code review (build-from-source instructions or a source zip note), not just a feature description
evals.notes: 2026-07-20 benchmark (1 run per config): with-skill 5/5, baseline 4/5. A strong base model already creates a root STORE.md, removes the unused activeTab, adds data_collection_permissions, and covers source-build notes unprompted; the discriminating assertion is per-store-sections-present (the heading-matched per-store shape that deploy's parser reads; baseline wrote one flat document with merged AMO/Edge notes and no release-notes section). Single-sample result; re-run before drawing variance conclusions.
